title: echinospartum ibericum subsp. ibericum
intro: Echinospartum ibericum subsp. ibericum, also known as Spanish Broom or Retama, is a flowering shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing high blood pressure, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Echinospartum ibericum subsp. ibericum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts have been studied for their potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
